It's essential that Mexico is held to its commitments under the USMCA to ensure lasting progress for our region. And if we walk away, we risk undoing hard-won progress and leave San Diego exposed to more pollution and economic setbacks. 3/3

The USMCA has been pivotal for San Diego, a city long plagued by cross-border pollution and chronic sewage spills largely caused by Mexico’s failing infrastructure, which threatens our beaches, public health, and local economy. A 🧵

In 2019, I voted to enact this agreement because it delivered $300 million to tackle the Tijuana River’s sewage crisis and upheld trade agreements supporting nearly 40,000 jobs in our region. Since then, we’ve secured even more funding, broken ground on new infrastructure,

Balboa Park is 1,200 acres of museums, trails, gardens, and recreational spaces. We must all do everything we can to support the Park and ensure residents and tourists can enjoy it for generations to come. Episode 5 of 72 and Sunny is all about this iconic jewel of San Diego.

14 years ago, President Obama first announced DACA and granted protections to people who came to the U.S. as children, some who have known no other home. 

Today, DACA recipients and other protected status holders make up 27,000 workers in San Diego and contribute $173 million in

Since 1777, the American flag has represented our country’s resilience and unity. As we prepare to celebrate America’s 250th birthday, #FlagDay is an important reminder of our founding ideals - democracy, freedom, and justice for all.
